rulings.Case Title: TRACI GRACE VS. ALVIN BAUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Baums Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday December 6, 2018 line 5. DEFENDANT ALVIN BAUM, ALVIN BAUM Defendant Baums Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Strike is GRANTED as to punitive damages and conspiracy allegations with leave to amend to allege facts supporting these claims with requisite specificity. Motion is otherwise DENIED. The remaining arguments are more appropriate on a properly noticed demurrer on motion for judgment on the pleadings as they address sufficiency of allegations. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TRACI GRACE VS. ALVIN BAUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday December 6, 2018 line 5. DEFENDANT ALVIN BAUM, ALVIN BAUM DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1110. Specifically Notice of Demurrer fails to state the nature of order being sought and grounds for demurrer. It is impossible to ascertain from the Notice whether demurrer is general or special, whether all causes of action are being demurred to, whether the demurrer is to the entire complaint or to specific causes of action, and if so on what grounds. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHOO KOON NG VS. DAMIEN QUINN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendants Second Amended Answer
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday December 6, 2018 line 7. PLAINTIFF CHOO NG Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Defendants Second Amended Answer GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to the second, third, fourth, fifth, seventh, and ninth affirmative defenses to the extent they are based on "use" and "zoning" issues. These issues have been conclusively resolved by the prior action involving the use and zoning of the same building under the same 2007 that has been modified (2010) and extended by option exercise (2013). Motion is denied as to the sixth defense, which in part is based on failure to re-rent/mitigate; and first and eighth affirmative defenses as no part of these defenses are based on "use" and "zoning." Additionally, this Court has not made any rulings on the merits of the proposed Second Amended Answer when motion for leave to amend was granted. = (501/REQ)
